resources.string: Choose the simple behavior for this session.
Use profile rules. Recommended for daily use.
Send all traffic through the selected server.

Unified delay — latency by real requests, not only TCP.
Geodata mode — local GeoIP/GeoSite for GEOIP/GEOSITE rules.
TCP concurrent — dial several resolved IPs and keep the fastest.
Mirrors for downloading the same geodata pack (or your own URLs)
Presets only change where files are downloaded from (CDN / host). It is still the same MetaCubeX/meta-rules-dat release — not separate “RU-only” or “CN-only” databases.
Custom: use the fields above; empty = built-in default for that slot.
Search active flows and close stale connections
App preferences, diagnostics and extra tools
Databases, meta features and core toggles
Theme, network, updates and advanced options
Dark mode, palette and text scale
Change app language without opening deep settings
HWID, clashfest:// scheme and copy buttons
Manage how traffic is routed: rule snippets, the active rule list, and per-app inclusion/exclusion.
Active routing rules from the current profile
Choose which apps go through the VPN tunnel
Show a banner on the main screen and a support button in About. Useful for operators distributing this build.
When on, the announcement appears in a card below the Active profile block. Turn off to show it under the title bar instead (compact layouts).
Short message shown on the main screen

Your per-app exclusion list is empty. Route installed Russian apps (banks, Госуслуги, marketplaces, carriers) directly through your ISP and keep them out of the VPN tunnel? You can edit the list later in Routing → Per-app.
The Quick Settings tile only starts or stops VPN; it does not change per-app routing.
%1$d Russian apps added to bypass list.
ClashFest can switch this screen to bypass mode and select installed Russian apps so they go directly through your ISP. You can edit the list after setup.
Don’t let the subscription server overwrite locally edited support URL or announcement.
Rules from the active profile (YAML order). In Rule mode, Clash walks this list top to bottom. This screen does not show live traffic.
For live hosts and proxy chains like Clash Verge, turn the VPN on and watch the core log while you use apps.
Edit routing rules for the active profile
Target proxy/group is missing in this profile
Edit the rule-providers block. Save applies a YAML preview to this profile.
Value is required for this rule type
Choose how you want to add your subscription.
Create a profile from a subscription URL already copied to the clipboard.
Open the screen where you can type or paste a subscription link.
Use the camera to read a subscription QR code.
Pick a local config file (.yaml) and import it as a profile.
Subscription updated, but your local edits could not be applied to the new config this time. Review them.
Disabled %1$d rule(s): their proxy/group no longer exists in the new subscription.
youtube.com — matches it and its subdomains
google — matches any domain containing this
Port must be 1–65535 (or a range like 8000-9000)
Pick a policy to see the rule line
Per-profile name resolution: DNS servers, fake-ip, and static host overrides.
Show the per-profile DNS & Hosts editor in Settings.
Activate a profile to edit its DNS & Hosts.
Manage DNS & Hosts for this profile
When on, your settings apply and survive subscription updates. Turning off reverts cleanly.
DNS & Hosts reverted for this profile.
DNS listen must be loopback (127.0.0.1:port), the default form (:port), or empty.
Per-profile static forwards: loopback listen → target via a proxy or group.
Show the per-profile Tunnels editor in Settings.
Activate a profile to edit its tunnels.
Unlock raw config override and other power-user knobs. These interact with the security hardening mode.
Raw clash config override (ports, listeners, DNS, hosts, mode).
When on, your tunnels apply and survive subscription updates. Turning off reverts cleanly.
Each entry listens on loopback and forwards traffic through the chosen proxy or group.
Loopback host:port only (e.g. 127.0.0.1:6553). Bare :port is not allowed.
Listen address must be an explicit loopback host:port.

Live flows from the core (VPN on). Totals are session counters; list refreshes about every second. Subtitle: process, Chain (policy path: selector / groups / leaf; [tag] is proxy-provider when present), network. dialer-proxy is not shown here — the core only records routing hops, not the extra hop used to dial a proxy server.
No matching connections — start traffic or clear the search filter.
Search host, destination, rule, proxy, app…
No request history yet — turn the VPN on and generate traffic.
No subscription link in clipboard (need http(s), content://, or mierus:// lines)
Connect a rules list from the web, or add one-off lines. Then copy the text into your profile (Edit / YAML).
Paste provider URL, confirm fields, preview result, then apply safely to existing rules.
Rule provider written to the active profile. Reloading…
Could not apply (no profile, or config already has rule-providers).
Ping needs a subscription URL profile
Headers ClashFest reads from your subscription URL response. Operators use them to brand the app, surface policies, and broadcast announcements.
Subscription / user name shown on the profile card.
Auto-update interval in hours (coerced to ≥15 min).
Per-subscription: hide share / copy-link actions and lock URL editing.
Device-binding state from the panel (active / not-supported / max-reached / limit).
Operator broadcast message + optional URL, surfaces as the home announcement card.
Legacy support URL (also Profile-Support-URL / Subscription-Support-URL). Surfaces in the announcement card support button; does not activate brand UI on its own.
Required master switch. Branding only applies when set to true; without it every X-Brand-* field is ignored.
App title shown in the main header and About sheet (≤32 chars).
One-line subtitle under the brand name (≤64 chars).
Light-theme logo variant, falls back to the primary on dark theme.
Material 3 seed for the harmonised palette (#RRGGBB).
Operator support contact. Use this instead of the legacy support-url.
Service status page (surfaces on connection issues).
Subscription renewal deep link. Tapping a critical-expiry chip opens it.
Personal account / billing deep link with a panel template variable (e.g. {{SHORT_UUID}} for a Telegram Mini App). Surfaces as the \"My account\" button on the Operator tab.
Per-user name shown in About (template-variable driven, e.g. {{USERNAME}}).
Hero greeting on the Operator tab. Combine template variables for dynamic content.
Explicit opt-in to add the dedicated Operator bottom-nav tab.
Paired with Show-Operator-Tab=true: replace Routing with Operator. No-op on its own.
No matching nodes. Clear search or filters.
This proxy is not listed under proxies: in the saved config (subscription-only name).
Nodes from subscription providers load in the engine after VPN starts. Turn the VPN on, then pick a real node here (not sub1/sub2 — those are provider keys).
Loading node list from the engine…
No nodes in this group yet. For groups built with filters, your subscription may have no matching proxies; otherwise wait a moment and tap the group chip again.
Delete this profile? This cannot be undone.
This subscription URL cannot be edited (operator policy).
Allow HTTP for subscription metadata probe
When off, metadata headers are only fetched over HTTPS. Enable only if your panel uses HTTP (cleartext).
Edit the rule-providers block. Save writes to the selected profile.
No providers in active profile yet.
Use the sync icon to refresh a single provider, or update them all. To disable a ruleset, open Rules -> Manual rules and turn off the matching RULE-SET line.
Turn the VPN on so the core can refresh rule providers
No remote rule providers loaded in the core yet
Manage current providers here. Add or edit in a focused creation sheet. Note: aggressive ads geosite lists may be unstable on some cores/devices.
GeoSite database is missing. Install/update geodata first for GEOSITE presets.
Preset added. If it does not match traffic, verify GeoSite key availability in current geodata build.
App lacks the necessary runtime components, which is usually caused by downloading an incomplete apk.
Use http(s), content://, or mierus:// share text
Profile needs to be saved before activation

An external link wants to add a subscription to ClashFest:

%1$s
%2$s

Only add subscriptions from sources you trust.
Auto routing all system traffic via VpnService
Protects against the SOCKS5/HTTP listener leak (a.k.a. VLESS-SOCKS5 vulnerability) that lets other apps on this device reach the proxy on 127.0.0.1.
Compat — keep listeners on 127.0.0.1 with random session credentials
If the imported subscription does not provide geox-url, ClashFest fills in default mirrors (MetaCubeX/Loyalsoldier). Fixes the «cant download geoip.dat» error.
Keep connections on old proxy after switch

By default ClashFest closes active connections after a manual proxy switch so new traffic uses the selected proxy immediately. Enable this to keep running connections on the previous proxy — useful for long downloads, video streams, or game sessions you don\'t want to interrupt.
Could not switch proxy. Existing connections were left untouched.
Proxy switched, but active connections could not be closed.
Developer mode: allows apps to bypass VPN. This can cause leak tests to fail.
Choose which apps should be routed through VPN
ClashFest for Android is a freeware and we do NOT provide any service for it
ClashFest on Telegram — bug reports & discussion
A new version is available. Download and install it now?
You already have the latest version
Could not download or install update
Allow installs from this source, then tap Check updates again.
Add your first subscription to start routing traffic and manage proxy groups.
Manage subscriptions, open editor, and sources from one place.
Optional. Applied only to this subscription (fetch/update/metadata). Leave empty to use default ClashFest UA.
On: fail immediately if this User-Agent returns invalid format. Off: auto-retry with default core User-Agent.
Only selected apps use the VPN
Add a subscription first, then tap Connect.
The selected subscription is not ready yet. Open subscriptions and save or update it.
Could not refresh now. Starting with the saved subscription.
Multiple proxy-providers (sub1, sub2, …) without editing YAML

Each row becomes proxy-providers sub1, sub2, … with path ./providers/subN.yaml. If you have two or more rows, each node name is prefixed with [subN] in the engine so the same name from two subscriptions cannot collide (otherwise the first subscription always wins). Health-check is enabled on each HTTP provider so delays show up for merged select groups. Labels are for you; keys in config are subN. This block is kept when the subscription URL refreshes. Use the ⋮ menu for merged groups. With VPN on, open Providers to refresh nodes. After adding a second row, save again and re-pick nodes (names change).
Choose another imported subscription with an HTTP(S) URL, then add it as a proxy-provider row (sub1, sub2, …).

Proxy-provider keys (sub1, sub2, …) are not node names — Mihomo loads nodes from each provider file. This screen adds a select group with use: [sub1, …] so the node you choose is what traffic uses. (A url-test group auto-picks the lowest latency to the test URL; if your pick does not pass that health check, the core falls back — often looking like “always the first subscription”.)

Relay groups were removed in this core; multi-hop uses dialer-proxy on proxy entries or Proxy chain in the Proxy tab.

Below are keys from your saved profile (after Save to profile).
No saved proxy-providers yet. Go back, add rows, and Save to profile.
No valid http(s) rows in saved proxy-providers.
Tap Save to profile on the previous screen so sub1, sub2, … exist in config.
Could not add: a group with this name already exists, or config is invalid.
Merged select group added. Use it in rules or Global; tap a node inside the group to switch servers. Remove and re-add if you still have an old url-test merged group.
None yet — add one below. You can remove a group here to clean up config.yaml.
This profile has no generated config yet.
Choose an action for this profile
Paste the subscription link, set a name and interval, then tap save.
Leave empty or 0 to disable; minimum 15 when enabled
Turn the VPN on to refresh providers from the core
Each row needs a valid http(s) URL
All override settings will be discarded
Config changed after preview generation. Regenerate the preview and try again.
Android VPN permission was not granted. Tap Connect again and allow the request.
Could not start VPN. Open logs to see the reason.
VPN permission was denied. Open ClashFest and allow the VPN request.
The settings have been reseted and the old profiles needs to be saved again.
Use an external GEOIP database file
Let other apps start, stop or toggle the VPN (e.g. Tasker, shortcuts). When off, external requests are ignored.
The VPN was stopped by an external request
You can dial *#*#252746382#*#* to open this App
Hide app from the Recent apps screen
Camera access is restricted. Please enable it in Settings.
Chain two proxies: pick the first hop (entry), then the exit. Traffic flows first hop → exit, and the exit is the IP you show online. The chain is written into the profile/provider YAML.
Use compatible transports (e.g. TCP/TLS with TCP/TLS). Mixing incompatible stacks often breaks the chain.
Save chain: write the chain only. Use now: also switch to Global and select the exit.
Writes the chain, switches the tunnel to Global mode and selects this proxy.
Writes the chain without changing tunnel mode or selection.
Saving the chain, reloading profile, switching to Global…
OK — Global mode, exit selected.\nExit: %1$s\nFirst hop: %2$s
Chain saved. Tunnel mode and selection were not changed.
Chain saved, but the proxy could not be selected under Global.
Turn the VPN on to use a chain (live proxy list required).
Start the VPN and load a profile first.
First hop and exit must be different.
Could not find that proxy in config or provider files.
No dialer-proxy entries in this profile.
All dialer-proxy fields removed. Config will reload.
Live proxy list is unavailable (VPN off or profile not loaded). Chains saved on disk are shown above — you can remove them without selecting proxies here.
Uses black surfaces in dark mode for OLED screens.
Control TV instances from your phone over the local network
This device can be controlled over the local network
Let paired devices on this network control this device. Off by default.
Scan this on the controlling device to pair
Review and revoke controllers paired with this device
Pair with another device by scanning its QR
Control devices you have paired with
Scan this code on the controlling device
Revoke %1$s? It will no longer be able to control this device.
No paired devices yet. Use \u201cScan QR to pair\u201d to add one.
Let your phone control this device over the local network
Pair and control a TV on your network
Pair a PC (e.g. SlothClash) by entering a code
Enter this PIN on the other device (valid 60s)
Starting remote control… try again in a moment
